text: Refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing how it works. Instead of adding new features, developers focus on making the code cleaner and easier to understand. This often means renaming confusing variables, breaking long functions into smaller parts, or removing duplicate logic. Good refactoring increases readability, which helps both the original author and other team members work with the code more efficiently. Avoiding refactoring leads to technical debt—a buildup of messy, poorly organized code that slows down development over time. By regularly refactoring, teams keep the codebase healthy, reduce future bugs, and make it simpler to add new features later.
